_fixture_note: ACTUAL output of the first live run (names/IDs kept generic where user-identifying). Historic detail: this run produced the first-ever live firing of snowflake-cost-audit's anomaly detector -- the +96.6% spike was caused by this project's own testing queries that day. Freshness staleness is true (static demo data). Used by fixture-test CI: structural assertions only. UPDATED after the fifth section (access_risks via snowflake-role-audit) was wired in: this file is again the ACTUAL output of a real combined run -- 11 findings across cluster/freshness/access sections, zero errors. The 6 access findings are genuine (single-user account holding ACCOUNTADMIN+ORGADMIN directly, privileged default role, real PUBLIC grants).

snowflake_cost.scope_note: Totals cover user-managed warehouses only (WAREHOUSE_METERING_HISTORY). Serverless features (tasks, Snowpipe, etc.) bill separately and are not included.

issues.recommendation: This is a config observation, not confirmed waste -- the cluster's auto-suspend is set well above 120 min, which *permits* long idle billing if left running unattended. Confirm with whoever owns cluster policy whether this is a deliberate tradeoff (e.g. personal clusters kept alive across a workday) before treating it as something to fix.

findings.recommendation: Confirm with whoever owns access policy whether this user needs standing access to this role, or whether it should be granted only when needed (e.g. via a lower-privileged default role with explicit role-switching).

Confirm with whoever owns access policy whether this user needs standing access to this role, or whether it should be granted only when needed (e.g. via a lower-privileged default role with explicit role-switching).

Every session for this user starts with this privileged role already active. Consider setting a lower-privileged default role and requiring an explicit USE ROLE to elevate, unless this is a deliberate choice for a small/trusted team.

findings.evidence: PUBLIC role holds OWNERSHIP on SCHEMA 'USER$ARSHATH.PUBLIC'
findings.recommendation: OWNERSHIP granted to PUBLIC means every user in the account can manage this object. Confirm with the account owner whether this is intentional (e.g. a shared sandbox schema) or a default that should be reassigned to a specific role.

findings.evidence: 73 non-ownership privileges granted directly to PUBLIC (e.g. ADD SEARCH OPTIMIZATION, APPLYBUDGET, CREATE AGENT, CREATE AGGREGATION POLICY, CREATE ALERT)
findings.recommendation: These grants apply to every user in the account via the PUBLIC role. Review whether this is the intended scope, or whether it should be a named role instead.
